channel.title: Last Day - NEW & UPDATE
channel.description: All NEW and UPDATE articles from the last day with enhanced metadata

item.title: NEW: LogiTrans Global Paralyzed by $45M QuantumLock Ransomware Attack, Global Supply Chains Disrupted
item.description:
Headline: LogiTrans Global Paralyzed by $45M QuantumLock Ransomware Attack, Global Supply Chains Disrupted

Summary:
Global logistics firm LogiTrans Global has been hit by a devastating ransomware attack attributed to a new group named QuantumLock. The attack, which began on June 30, 2026, has encrypted critical systems, causing a worldwide halt in operations, including shipment tracking and fleet management. The attackers gained initial access via a compromised VPN, exploited a known vulnerability (CVE-2026-23456) for privilege escalation, and exfiltrated 5 TB of sensitive data before deploying the ransomware. A $45 million ransom is being demanded, with the threat of leaking stolen data. The incident has caused severe disruption to the global supply chain, and LogiTrans has engaged Mandiant and the FBI to manage the crisis.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Supply Chain Attack

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 6 minutes

Meta Description: A new ransomware group, QuantumLock, has crippled logistics giant LogiTrans Global in a massive cyberattack, demanding $45 million and disrupting global supply chains.

Article Slug: logitrans-global-paralyzed-by-quantumlock-ransomware

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Actively Exploited 'ShiftScribe' Zero-Day in Apex-Office Suite Puts Millions at Risk
item.description:
Headline: Actively Exploited 'ShiftScribe' Zero-Day in Apex-Office Suite Puts Millions at Risk

Summary:
A critical remote code execution (RCE) zero-day vulnerability, named 'ShiftScribe' and tracked as CVE-2026-35801, has been discovered in the popular Apex-Office Suite, affecting over 100 million users. The flaw, with a CVSS score of 9.6, is being actively exploited by the state-sponsored threat actor 'Gilded Moth' (APT42) in targeted attacks against government and defense organizations. The attack involves spear-phishing emails with malicious documents that trigger the vulnerability, allowing attackers to deploy a custom backdoor called 'Inkwell'. CISA has added the CVE to its KEV catalog and issued an emergency directive, while Apex Software rushes to develop a patch.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Cyberattack

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: A critical RCE zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-35801) in Apex-Office Suite is being actively exploited by state-sponsored actors to deploy malware. CISA has issued an emergency directive.

Article Slug: critical-zero-day-shiftscribe-in-apex-office-suite-under-active-exploitation

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: HealthNet Insurance Breach Exposes Sensitive Health Data of 11 Million Patients via Misconfigured AWS S3 Bucket
item.description:
Headline: HealthNet Insurance Breach Exposes Sensitive Health Data of 11 Million Patients via Misconfigured AWS S3 Bucket

Summary:
HealthNet Insurance, a major US health provider, has disclosed a data breach that exposed the personal and medical information of 11 million individuals. The breach, discovered on June 15, 2026, originated from a misconfigured Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3 bucket that contained access keys to other sensitive databases. Attackers gained access around March 3, 2026, and were able to exfiltrate a vast amount of data, including Social Security numbers, medical claims history, and diagnosis codes. The incident has triggered class-action lawsuits and regulatory investigation by the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS).

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Cloud Security, Regulatory

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: HealthNet Insurance discloses a massive data breach affecting 11 million patients after a misconfigured AWS S3 bucket exposed sensitive personal and medical data.

Article Slug: healthnet-insurer-breach-exposes-data-of-11-million-patients

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: 'SandViper' APT Hits Global Energy Sector with New 'DuneStalker' Espionage Malware
item.description:
Headline: 'SandViper' APT Hits Global Energy Sector with New 'DuneStalker' Espionage Malware

Summary:
A new report from Dragos Inc. details a sophisticated cyber-espionage campaign targeting the global energy sector by a previously unknown threat actor named 'SandViper'. The campaign, active since late 2025, focuses on oil and gas companies in the Middle East and Europe. SandViper uses a novel modular backdoor called 'DuneStalker' to infiltrate networks and steal sensitive operational technology (OT) data, including geological surveys and production metrics. The initial access is gained through spear-phishing and watering hole attacks. While not definitively attributed, the TTPs show similarities to Russian and Iranian APT groups.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Malware, Industrial Control Systems

Article Type: Report

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: A new APT group, SandViper, is targeting the global energy sector with a novel modular backdoor called DuneStalker to steal sensitive operational technology (OT) data.

Article Slug: sandviper-apt-targets-global-energy-sector-with-dunestalker-malware

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: CodeStream CI/CD Utility Compromised in Supply Chain Attack, Developer Secrets Stolen
item.description:
Headline: CodeStream CI/CD Utility Compromised in Supply Chain Attack, Developer Secrets Stolen

Summary:
The popular open-source CI/CD utility 'CodeStream' has been the target of a sophisticated supply chain attack. An unknown threat actor compromised the project's package registry, publishing a malicious version (3.4.1) on June 29, 2026. The tainted version, downloaded over 50,000 times, was designed to scan and exfiltrate sensitive credentials, API keys, and tokens from developer environments for services like AWS, GitHub, and Artifactory. The CodeStream team has released a clean version (3.4.2) and is urging all users to upgrade immediately, rotate all credentials, and audit their systems for compromise.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Security Operations, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: A supply chain attack on the CodeStream CI/CD utility resulted in a malicious version (3.4.1) that steals developer secrets. Users are urged to upgrade and rotate all credentials.

Article Slug: supply-chain-attack-malicious-code-injected-into-codestream-cicd-utility

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Microsoft Releases Emergency Patch for Actively Exploited Exchange Server RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-17747)
item.description:
Headline: Microsoft Releases Emergency Patch for Actively Exploited Exchange Server RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-17747)

Summary:
Microsoft has released an emergency, out-of-band security update for a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange Server 2016 and 2019. Tracked as CVE-2026-17747 with a CVSS score of 9.8, the flaw allows an unauthenticated attacker to gain code execution by sending a specially crafted email to a vulnerable server. The vulnerability is being actively exploited in limited, targeted attacks. In response, CISA has added it to its KEV catalog and issued an emergency directive, mandating federal agencies to patch their servers within 48 hours. All organizations running on-premises Exchange are urged to apply the update immediately.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Article Type: Advisory

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: Microsoft has released an emergency out-of-band patch for a critical, actively exploited RCE vulnerability (CVE-2026-17747) in Exchange Server 2016 and 2019. Patch immediately.

Article Slug: microsoft-issues-emergency-patch-for-exchange-server-rce-flaw-cve-2026-17747

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: 'FinReact' Phishing Campaign Uses AI-Generated Lures and 'GhostScript' Loader to Target Banks
item.description:
Headline: 'FinReact' Phishing Campaign Uses AI-Generated Lures and 'GhostScript' Loader to Target Banks

Summary:
A highly sophisticated phishing campaign named 'FinReact' is targeting the financial services industry. Researchers at Proofpoint report that the campaign uses generative AI to create convincing, context-aware email lures that bypass traditional security. The attack chain involves a password-protected ZIP file containing a malicious LNK file, which downloads a novel JavaScript loader called 'GhostScript'. This loader performs anti-sandbox checks before deploying information-stealing malware like Vidar and IcedID. The campaign is attributed to a skilled, financially motivated threat actor.

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 6 minutes

Meta Description: A new phishing campaign, FinReact, is using generative AI and a novel JavaScript loader called GhostScript to target the financial sector and deploy info-stealing malware.

Article Slug: finreact-phishing-campaign-targets-financial-sector-with-ai-lures

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: DataHaven Cloud Storage Outage Caused by Security Breach Targeting Customer Data
item.description:
Headline: DataHaven Cloud Storage Outage Caused by Security Breach Targeting Customer Data

Summary:
Enterprise cloud storage provider DataHaven has confirmed that a multi-hour global outage on June 30, 2026, was the result of a security breach. Attackers gained access to an internal Kubernetes cluster via a zero-day vulnerability in a proprietary management API. They then attempted to issue commands to delete customer data. DataHaven's automated safety protocols detected the malicious activity and initiated a global lockdown of the control plane to prevent data loss. While this action was successful in protecting data, it inadvertently caused a cascading service-wide failure, highlighting the systemic risks in centralized cloud services.

Severity: High

Categories: Cloud Security, Cyberattack, Incident Response

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: Cloud storage provider DataHaven suffered a major global outage after a security breach of its internal Kubernetes management API, where attackers attempted to delete customer data.

Article Slug: cloud-storage-provider-datahaven-suffers-outage-after-breach

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: EU Unveils 'CYBER-AI Act' with Strict Security Mandates and Heavy Fines for High-Risk AI
item.description:
Headline: EU Unveils 'CYBER-AI Act' with Strict Security Mandates and Heavy Fines for High-Risk AI

Summary:
The European Commission has introduced the 'Cybersecurity of Artificial Intelligence Act' (CYBER-AI Act), a new regulation that imposes stringent cybersecurity requirements on high-risk AI systems. Working alongside the existing AI Act, this legislation mandates secure-by-design principles, threat modeling, penetration testing, and vulnerability management for AI developers and deployers. Key requirements include maintaining a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) and reporting significant incidents to ENISA within 24 hours. Non-compliance can result in massive fines of up to €30 million or 6% of global turnover.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: The EU has introduced the 'CYBER-AI Act', a new regulation that sets strict cybersecurity standards for high-risk AI systems, with heavy fines for non-compliance.

Article Slug: eu-announces-strict-ai-security-regulations-in-cyber-ai-act

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: 'Synapse' Wiper Malware Disguised as Ransomware, Designed for Maximum Destruction
item.description:
Headline: 'Synapse' Wiper Malware Disguised as Ransomware, Designed for Maximum Destruction

Summary:
Kaspersky researchers have published a detailed analysis of 'Synapse,' a new and highly destructive malware strain. Initially mistaken for ransomware, Synapse is a pure wiper designed to cause irreversible data loss. It uses deception by dropping a fake ransom note and encrypting a few non-essential files, while its main component destroys the Master Boot Record (MBR) and overwrites file headers with junk data. The attack vector in the analyzed case was a compromised software update. The wiper shows code overlaps with malware attributed to the 'Sandworm' APT group, suggesting a possible link to the sophisticated state-sponsored actor.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Malware, Threat Actor, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: Report

Reading Time: 6 minutes

Meta Description: Kaspersky researchers analyze Synapse, a new destructive wiper malware that masquerades as ransomware to cause irreversible data damage, with possible links to the Sandworm APT.

Article Slug: deep-dive-analysis-of-synapse-wiper-malware-posing-as-ransomware

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: One Year Later: Omni Hotels Details $50M Recovery and Security Overhaul After Devastating Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Headline: One Year Later: Omni Hotels Details $50M Recovery and Security Overhaul After Devastating Ransomware Attack

Summary:
Omni Hotels & Resorts has published a detailed report outlining its recovery and security transformation one year after a catastrophic ransomware attack in 2025. The incident, attributed to the BlackSuit gang, cost the company over $50 million and led to a complete overhaul of its cybersecurity infrastructure. The hotel chain invested $35 million in a new security stack based on zero-trust principles, including network micro-segmentation, advanced EDR, and phishing-resistant MFA. The report serves as a case study in incident response, recovery, and the importance of board-level engagement in cybersecurity.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Incident Response, Security Operations, Ransomware

Article Type: Analysis

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: One year after a devastating $50 million ransomware attack, Omni Hotels & Resorts shares details of its recovery and complete security overhaul based on zero-trust principles.

Article Slug: omni-hotels-recovery-one-year-after-2025-breach

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: AI-Powered Disinformation is Top Threat to 2028 Elections, Mandiant Warns
item.description:
Headline: AI-Powered Disinformation is Top Threat to 2028 Elections, Mandiant Warns

Summary:
A new report from Mandiant (part of Google Cloud) identifies AI-powered disinformation and influence operations as the most significant cyber threat to the 2028 U.S. election cycle. The report warns of a future filled with hyper-realistic deepfakes and AI-driven social media manipulation designed to sow discord and influence voters. Researchers highlight the democratization of these tools, with 'Disinformation-as-a-Service' (DaaS) platforms emerging on the dark web. Mandiant urges a collaborative effort between tech platforms, government, and the public to develop new countermeasures.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Other

Article Type: Report

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: A new Mandiant report warns that AI-powered disinformation and influence operations, including deepfakes and 'Disinformation-as-a-Service', are the top threat to the 2028 U.S. election.

Article Slug: mandiant-report-ai-powered-disinformation-top-threat-for-2028-elections

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Phantom Squatting: Attackers Weaponize AI-Hallucinated Domains in Novel Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
Headline: Phantom Squatting: Attackers Weaponize AI-Hallucinated Domains in Novel Supply Chain Attacks

Summary:
Cybersecurity researchers from Unit 42 have identified a new and active software supply chain attack vector dubbed 'Phantom Squatting.' The threat involves adversaries exploiting the tendency of Large Language Models (LLMs) to 'hallucinate,' or invent, non-existent web domains for legitimate brands. Attackers are preemptively registering these phantom domains to intercept traffic, deploy malware, and execute phishing campaigns. A notable case involved the 'Montana Empire' phishing kit being deployed on a domain that researchers had predicted would be a hallucination target 23 days prior. The study analyzed over 2.1 million URLs generated by LLMs, uncovering 13,229 malicious URLs and identifying approximately 250,000 unregistered phantom domains, representing a massive and exploitable attack surface. This method effectively creates a 'zero-reputation bypass,' as the malicious infrastructure is brand new and appears authoritative to developers and automated systems that trust the LLM's output.

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Threat Intelligence, Malware

Article Type: Report

Keywords: phantom squatting, AI security, LLM hallucination, software supply chain attack, Unit 42, Montana Empire, CI/CD security, malicious domains, threat research

Reading Time: 19 minutes

Meta Description: Unit 42 research reveals 'Phantom Squatting,' a novel software supply chain attack vector where adversaries exploit LLM-hallucinated domains to conduct phishing and intercept sensitive data.

Article Slug: phantom-squatting-ai-hallucinated-domains-as-a-software-supply-chain-vector

Published Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: SimpleHelp RMM Flaw Actively Exploited to Deploy Novel Djinn Stealer
item.description:
Headline: SimpleHelp RMM Flaw Actively Exploited to Deploy Novel Djinn Stealer

Summary:
A critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-48558, in SimpleHelp remote support software is being actively exploited to gain administrative access to servers. Attackers are leveraging this 10.0 CVSS flaw to deploy a new, cross-platform information stealer called Djinn Stealer via a loader named TaskWeaver. The vulnerability allows attackers to create privileged accounts, bypassing MFA, and use the RMM's trusted channels to compromise all connected endpoints. CISA has added the flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, mandating urgent mitigation for federal agencies.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Malware, Cyberattack

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: A critical 10.0 CVSS authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-48558) in SimpleHelp RMM software is being actively exploited to deploy Djinn Stealer malware. Learn about the attack and mitigation steps.

Article Slug: attackers-exploit-simplehelp-cve-2026-48558-to-deploy-djinn-stealer

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Microsoft Dismantles "StegoAd," a Malicious Edge Extension Campaign Using Steganography
item.description:
Headline: Microsoft Dismantles "StegoAd," a Malicious Edge Extension Campaign Using Steganography

Summary:
Microsoft has disrupted a major malicious browser extension campaign named 'StegoAd' that affected its Edge Add-ons store for over two years. The operation involved 119 extensions, downloaded by up to 2.6 million users, which used steganography to hide malicious JavaScript payloads within image and font files. Initially performing ad fraud, the malware also had backdoor capabilities to steal credentials from Google and WordPress, as well as exfiltrate browser cookies for session hijacking. The campaign is linked to a Chinese threat actor known as DarkSpectre.

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Supply Chain Attack, Phishing

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Learn about the 'StegoAd' campaign where 119 malicious Microsoft Edge extensions used steganography to infect 2.6 million users, stealing credentials and committing ad fraud.

Article Slug: microsoft-disrupts-stegoad-malicious-edge-extension-campaign

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: "GuardFall" Flaw Lets Old Bash Tricks Bypass Modern AI Agent Security
item.description:
Headline: "GuardFall" Flaw Lets Old Bash Tricks Bypass Modern AI Agent Security

Summary:
A new class of vulnerability named 'GuardFall' has been discovered, affecting ten out of eleven popular open-source AI coding agents. The flaw allows attackers to bypass the agents' security guards using decades-old Bash shell obfuscation tricks. By crafting malicious text in a file like a README, an attacker can trick the AI agent into executing destructive or data-stealing commands with the developer's full permissions. This exposes developers to severe supply chain attacks, credential theft, and environment destruction, highlighting a fundamental design flaw in how these agents validate commands.

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Supply Chain Attack, Other

Article Type: Analysis

Meta Description: Researchers detail 'GuardFall,' a new class of vulnerability in open-source AI coding agents that allows old Bash shell tricks to bypass security, enabling supply chain attacks.

Article Slug: guardfall-vulnerability-exposes-ai-coding-agents-to-bash-tricks

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: UK Police Launch Campaign as Ransomware Attacks on Businesses Surge
item.description:
Headline: UK Police Launch Campaign as Ransomware Attacks on Businesses Surge

Summary:
Authorities in the United Kingdom, led by the City of London Police, have launched a national campaign to combat the rising tide of ransomware. New statistics reveal that 323 UK organizations reported ransomware attacks between April 2025 and March 2026, with over half being small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). Reported financial losses increased by 50% to £270,000, though officials believe the true cost is much higher due to underreporting. The campaign urges businesses not to pay ransoms and to focus on proactive defenses like backups and system updates.

Severity: Medium

Categories: Ransomware, Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: UK police and Report Fraud launch a national campaign against ransomware, revealing 323 businesses were attacked last year. Learn why they advise not to pay the ransom.

Article Slug: uk-police-launch-campaign-as-ransomware-attacks-on-businesses-surge

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Critical Pre-Auth RCE Flaw in libssh2 Library Poses Widespread Risk
item.description:
Headline: Critical Pre-Auth RCE Flaw in libssh2 Library Poses Widespread Risk

Summary:
A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability, CVE-2026-55200, has been disclosed in libssh2, a widely used open-source SSH client library. The flaw, rated 9.8 on the CVSS scale, is a pre-authentication heap buffer overflow that can be triggered by a malicious SSH server. Because libssh2 is embedded in countless software products—including curl, Git clients, and IoT devices—the vulnerability represents a massive supply chain risk. Public proof-of-concept code is available, increasing the likelihood of mass exploitation.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Supply Chain Attack

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: A critical 9.8 CVSS pre-authentication RCE vulnerability (CVE-2026-55200) in the libssh2 library poses a massive supply chain risk to software like curl, Git, and more. Learn about the risk and mitigations.

Article Slug: critical-pre-auth-rce-flaw-cve-2026-55200-in-libssh2-library

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: EDPB Adopts Common Template for GDPR Data Breach Notifications
item.description:
Headline: EDPB Adopts Common Template for GDPR Data Breach Notifications

Summary:
The European Data Protection Board (EDPB) has adopted a common, standardized template for organizations to use when notifying supervisory authorities of a personal data breach under the GDPR. This initiative aims to simplify the compliance process and harmonize the currently fragmented reporting requirements across different EU member states. The unified form is expected to reduce administrative burdens, especially for smaller organizations, by providing a clear structure and predefined values. The template is now open for public consultation until August 5, 2026.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Data Breach

Article Type: Report

Meta Description: The European Data Protection Board (EDPB) adopts a common template for GDPR data breach notifications to streamline and harmonize reporting requirements across the EU.

Article Slug: edpb-adopts-common-template-for-gdpr-data-breach-notifications

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Polymarket Loses $3M in Supply Chain Attack on Frontend Vendor
item.description:
Headline: Polymarket Loses $3M in Supply Chain Attack on Frontend Vendor

Summary:
The cryptocurrency prediction market Polymarket has lost approximately $3 million in customer funds due to a supply chain attack. Threat actors compromised a third-party JavaScript vendor used by the platform, allowing them to inject a malicious script into Polymarket's website. This script tricked 11 users into approving fraudulent transactions, which drained their wallets. The attack highlights the significant risks posed by third-party dependencies in web applications. Polymarket has promised to fully refund all affected users.

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Cyberattack, Data Breach

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Polymarket, a crypto prediction market, suffered a $3 million loss after a third-party JavaScript vendor was compromised, leading to a frontend supply chain attack that drained user funds.

Article Slug: polymarket-loses-3m-in-supply-chain-attack-on-frontend-vendor

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Russian APT Gamaredon Enhances Evasion via Cloud Services
item.description:
Headline: Russian APT Gamaredon Enhances Evasion via Cloud Services

Summary:
The Russian state-sponsored threat group Gamaredon (aka Aqua Blizzard) has significantly evolved its tactics to improve evasion and scale its cyber-espionage campaigns, primarily targeting Ukraine. New research shows the group is abusing legitimate cloud services like Cloudflare, Dropbox, and AWS for command-and-control (C2) to hide its traffic. Gamaredon has also developed new malware, including the PteroPaste USB spreader. Notably, the group has been observed collaborating with the Turla APT, using its widespread access to deploy Turla's more sophisticated Kazuar malware as a second-stage payload.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Cyberattack, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: Analysis

Meta Description: Russian state-sponsored APT group Gamaredon (Aqua Blizzard) is evolving, using legitimate cloud services for C2 and collaborating with Turla to attack Ukrainian targets.

Article Slug: russian-apt-gamaredon-enhances-evasion-via-cloud-services

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: New Email Attacks Use Real Microsoft Login Pages to Bypass MFA
item.description:
Headline: New Email Attacks Use Real Microsoft Login Pages to Bypass MFA

Summary:
Security researchers are warning about increasingly sophisticated email attacks, including the Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform. This adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) attack uses a reverse proxy to present the victim with a real Microsoft login page. When the user authenticates and completes the MFA challenge, the platform intercepts the session token, allowing the attacker to gain full access to the user's Microsoft 365 account, completely bypassing conventional MFA protection. The attack highlights the growing threat of PhaaS and the need for phishing-resistant MFA.

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: Analysis

Meta Description: Learn how the Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform uses adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) attacks to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 accounts.

Article Slug: new-email-attacks-use-real-microsoft-login-pages-to-bypass-mfa

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: TUANZ Calls for "Security by Design" in New Zealand's Digital Future
item.description:
Headline: TUANZ Calls for "Security by Design" in New Zealand's Digital Future

Summary:
The Tech Users Association of New Zealand (TUANZ) has published a new position paper urging a fundamental shift in the nation's cybersecurity strategy. The group is calling for a move away from the current 'user beware' model, which places a heavy burden on individuals and SMEs. Instead, TUANZ advocates for embedding 'security by design' principles into all digital systems and establishing stronger accountability for technology providers. The paper warns that the growing complexity of cyber threats is creating an 'equity gap,' leaving those without resources dangerously exposed.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Security Operations

Article Type: Report

Meta Description: The Tech Users Association of New Zealand (TUANZ) releases a position paper calling for a national cybersecurity strategy based on 'security by design' to protect users and SMEs.

Article Slug: tuanz-calls-for-security-by-design-in-new-zealands-digital-future

Published Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Russian APT Gamaredon Enhances Malware and Evasion Techniques in Ukraine War
item.description:
Headline: Russian APT Gamaredon Enhances Malware and Evasion Techniques in Ukraine War

Summary:
The Russian APT group Gamaredon has continued to evolve its malware arsenal and evasion tactics in its relentless cyber campaign against Ukraine. Recent analysis shows the group is increasingly using legitimate cloud services like Cloudflare for C2, collaborating with other Russian APTs like Turla, and using new malware variants like PteroSand. The campaigns, primarily targeting Ukrainian government and military entities, focus on espionage and data exfiltration.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Cyberattack, Malware

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: Russian APT group Gamaredon has enhanced its cyberattacks against Ukraine, using legitimate cloud services for C2, collaborating with other APTs like Turla, and deploying new malware.

Article Slug: russian-apt-gamaredon-evolves-tactics-in-attacks-on-ukraine

Published Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 12:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →
